title: rootless: do not enable lingering mode
body:
do not automatically enable lingering mode.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/4224

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
/approve

BTW, even if a user has lingering enabled, that doesn't mean they want all libpod containers to linger.  Or to say it another way, there should probably be an option like `--bind-to-session` or something to have them die with the last session.  (Think about the case of running some desktop app inside a podman container)

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cgwalters*, *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@giuseppe Why not add a `podman create --linger` flag and leave the code, then users could create containers that remain running, if the user session is set to linger.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #4279) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

rebased.


> @giuseppe Why not add a `podman create --linger` flag and leave the code, then users could create containers that remain running, if the user session is set to linger.

if the user session is configured to linger, then the containers remain running also now.

The original code was doing it silently, to behave more like root users.  IMO, an additional command flag is equivalent to suggest using `loginctl enable-linger $UID`.
